research_topics: Multi-agent LLM debate for trading decisions
Kalman-enhanced Deep RL for noise resilience
Dynamic role assignment in multi-agent systems

findings.finding: XGBoost + FinBERT sentiment + regime filtering achieved 67% accuracy vs 54% baseline. Key: dynamically adjusting market exposure based on volatility and return filtering eliminates false signals in non-trending regimes.
findings.relevance_to_us: Direct application. Our system has 29 signals but no dynamic exposure adjustment. We could apply regime-conditioned signal gating: in ranging markets, disable trend/momentum signals with <45% accuracy.

findings.finding: Multi-model Signal Fabric with regime classification + stability-constrained Meta-Learner Spine. Continuous adjustment of signal relevance as volatility clustering and correlation dynamics evolve.
findings.relevance_to_us: We have meta-learner models. Missing piece: regime-conditioned weight adjustment. Current weights are static. AlphaGen shows stability constraints prevent overfitting during regime transitions.

findings.finding: Test signals using Information Coefficient (IC) - rank correlation between signal and future returns. Only use signals with stable, positive IC. Rolling IC windows detect signal decay.
findings.relevance_to_us: Critical. We use accuracy (binary hit rate) but IC is more informative. A signal can be 50% accurate but have positive IC if correct calls have larger moves. Should supplement accuracy gating with IC.

findings.finding: Hidden Markov Models identify market states (trending/ranging/volatile), then Random Forest selects optimal signal subset per regime.
findings.relevance_to_us: Direct fit. We detect regimes but dont use it to filter signals. HMM is more sophisticated than ADX-based detection. Could train on signal_log.db.

findings.finding: Bull/bear debate architecture. Specialized agents argue positions. Now supports Claude 4.x. Fundamental analyst, sentiment expert, technical analyst, trader, risk manager collaborate.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our Layer 2 is single Claude call. Bull/bear debate could improve decision quality. Deferred - would increase CLI usage significantly.

findings.finding: Momentum-specific indicator fusion dynamically weights VWAP, MACD, RSI based on volume confirmation and ML probability scores.
findings.relevance_to_us: We have volume as separate signal. Fusion approach weights other signals higher WHEN volume confirms. More nuanced than binary RVOL gate.

findings.finding: Kalman filter applied to noisy signals reduces whipsaw. Filtered signal has smoother transitions, fewer false crossovers.
findings.relevance_to_us: Could apply to MACD, EMA crossovers that flip rapidly. Adds persistence without arbitrary lookback. Addresses known issue of single-cycle noise.

recommended_improvements.title: Regime-Conditioned Signal Gating
recommended_improvements.description: In ranging regimes, force-HOLD signals with <45% accuracy in that regime. Trend at 40.3% (3h) should be gated. Per-regime thresholds.

recommended_improvements.title: IC-Based Signal Weighting
recommended_improvements.description: Calculate rolling 7-day IC per signal. Weight by IC magnitude. Negative IC = force-HOLD. More informative than binary accuracy.

recommended_improvements.title: Volume-Conditional Confidence Boost
recommended_improvements.description: RVOL > 1.5x: boost confirming signals 1.2x. RVOL < 0.5x: reduce all confidence 0.6x. Graduated, not binary.

recommended_improvements.title: Signal Persistence Filter
recommended_improvements.description: Require 2+ consecutive cycles for a signal to count as vote. Eliminates single-cycle noise flips.
